summary: Fiery set with scorching Tennessee Jed, hot jams and vocal rap
blurb: The second set erupts with a trippy Tennessee Jed → Cumberland Blues opener and a blazing I Need A Miracle > Crazy Fingers segue that leads into an extended post‑drum jam. Highlights include a hot Comes A Time into Gimme Some Lovin' featuring extra Phil, plus strong vocal rap moments from Phil and Brent.
review: Long Beach Arena on November 16, 1985 delivered a compact but incendiary performance that still feels fresh for Deadheads. The first set kicked off with the thunderous “Hell In A Bucket” and rolled through classics like “Sugaree,” “El Paso,” and “Dupree's Diamond Blues,” keeping the energy high throughout. The real fireworks began in Set 2: a surprising Tennessee Jed → Cumberland Blues opening set the tone, then the band launched into an electrifying I Need A Miracle → Crazy Fingers transition that spiraled into a massive 27‑minute pre‑drum jam and a post‑drum explosion. “Comes A Time” melted seamlessly into a rollicking “Gimme Some Lovin’” where Phil added extra vocal fire, while Jerry’s guitar tone stayed razor‑sharp, even cutting the jam to drive straight into a rousing “Truckin'.” The night closed with solid renditions of “Black Peter,” “Good Lovin’,” and an upbeat encore of “Keep Your Day Job.” Across all recordings the show is praised for its high‑octane playing, vocal rap moments from Phil (joined by Brent), and a tight rhythm section that kept the groove burning to the very end.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, highlighting scorching Tennessee Jed, hot jams, vocal rap and strong post‑drum sections; the third is mixed but neutral. The consistent praise across multiple sources warrants a solid positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard tape, clean and balanced mix, captures the full set with all standout moments and minimal technical issues.

key_highlights: Tennessee Jed → Cumberland Blues opener that set a trippy second‑set tone
I Need A Miracle > Crazy Fingers segue feeding into an extended post‑drum jam
Comes A Time into a fiery Gimme Some Lovin' with extra Phil vocals
Vocal rap moments from Phil and Brent, plus Jerry’s sharp guitar throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone is praised throughout; he cuts the jam cleanly to launch into ‘Truckin'’ and stays on fire for the whole night.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers strong vocal rap with Brent, adds extra vocals/guitar on ‘Gimme Some Lovin’’, and provides a solid bass foundation that locks in with the drums.
band_performance.Brent: Highlights include his vocal contributions in the closing rap and the extra Phil‑style harmonies on ‘Gimme Some Lovin’’.
band_performance.Mickey: Both drummers create a powerful post‑drum jam after the Crazy Fingers segment, driving the energy forward.
band_performance.Billy: Shares in the massive pre‑drum and post‑drum jams, keeping the rhythmic pulse tight and explosive.
